overview.narrative: Over the last 7 days, 1,713 contacts reached a human. We classify 1,090 (64%) as rightful escalations — exceptions, special-handling, and emotionally hard contacts a bot shouldn't contain. After handoff your team recovered 71% of them; the rest ended worse than they arrived. Recovery is breaking most on special-handling exceptions (like customers traveling with medical equipment) — and three agents recover them far better than the rest.

kpis.label: Rightful Escalations
kpis.sub: 64% of handovers · correctly needed a human

recommendedActions.title: Coach recovery on special-handling exceptions
recommendedActions.desc: 62% recovery vs 88% for your top recoverers. Same hard contacts — the gap is human handling, not the bot.

recommendedActions.title: Arm agents with the special-handling exception path
recommendedActions.desc: Agents stall on who can authorize a partner-carrier or equipment-handling exception. Push the path + precedent into Agent Assist.

recommendedActions.title: Cut the 48s wait before pickup on hot transfers
recommendedActions.desc: Hot, rightful escalations wait 48s in silence before pickup — 9% abandon mid-transfer.

playbook.intro: These escalation flows are losing the most customers after handoff. Pick one to fix — see who recovers it best, what they actually say, then publish reusable guidance into Agent Assist so every agent gets it.

flows.name: Special-handling exception

examples.quote: Because you travel with medical equipment, I've already arranged a partner-carrier exception and flagged temperature-controlled handling at both airports — set up just for you.
I can see you need special handling — I've personally called ground crew so your equipment is prioritised on and off the aircraft.
I've documented this as a standing exception on your profile, so every future trip is handled this way without you asking.

flows.commonMove: name the special need → personalise it → arrange it proactively (never "it's in the notes")

guidance.template: Because you travel with [special-need], I've arranged a partner-carrier exception on [rebooked-flight] and actioned assistance with [assistance-type] with ground crews at [origin] and [destination] — set up specifically for you.
guidance.surface: Copilot for Agents · a suggested response the agent can insert
guidance.authoredIn: GenAI Prompt Editor · Draft → Test → Publish (versioned)

flows.name: Out-of-policy approval

examples.quote: I've applied a one-time exception and authorised this for you — it's done, you won't need to call back.
This is outside standard policy, so I've personally approved it given your history with us.

flows.commonMove: name the exception as personal and already done — not "let me check if I can"

guidance.template: I've applied a [exception-type] exception and authorised [resolution] for you — it's done. I've noted [account] so this is handled going forward.
guidance.surface: Copilot for Agents · a suggested response the agent can insert
guidance.authoredIn: GenAI Prompt Editor · Draft → Test → Publish (versioned)
guidance.surfaceOn: Out-of-policy approval escalations · skill = Billing / Retention

flows.name: Manager / authority sign-off

examples.quote: I've looped in my supervisor and we've agreed on this together — here's exactly what happens next and when.

flows.commonMove: give a concrete next step + timeline so the wait never feels open-ended

guidance.template: I've escalated this to [authority] and we've agreed [decision]. Here's what happens next: [next-step] by [timeframe].
guidance.surface: Copilot for Agents · a suggested response the agent can insert
guidance.authoredIn: GenAI Prompt Editor · Draft → Test → Publish (versioned)

transcript.text: My flight MSP→CDG was cancelled and I travel with powered medical equipment that needs temperature-controlled handling. I have to get there tomorrow.
I'm sorry — flight VYR221 was cancelled due to the storm. Rebooking onto a partner carrier and the medical-equipment handling both need a specialist to authorize. I'm connecting you to one now.
Okay, I found a seat on EuroAir EA402 tomorrow evening and rebooked you. Anything else?
But what about my medical equipment? Will it actually be handled? Last time it sat on the tarmac and nearly failed. I'm really worried.
It should be fine — it's in the booking notes. You're all set.
That's it? This is exactly what went wrong last time. I need to speak to someone else.

assist.classification: Rightful escalation — partner-carrier + medical-equipment exception. A bot can't authorize this. Goal: recover the relationship.

facts.label: Policy
facts.value: Medical equipment: flag priority offload + temperature-controlled handling with ground services

assist.gap: QM will score Tom's agent skills as usual — but the recovery across this handoff isn't stitched or scored anywhere today. Tom rebooked the flight yet never addressed the equipment, so sentiment never recovered and Alex re-escalated. The best-recoverer move that fixes this isn't in Agent Assist yet.
assist.publishedGuidance: Because you travel with medical equipment, I've arranged a partner-carrier exception on EuroAir EA402 and personally arranged assistance with temperature-controlled handling with ground crews at MSP and CDG — set up specifically for you.
